You are executing a bridled pace for the Job Jockey system. Your task is a full cross-regime audit of variable voicings.

## Docket

Full cross-regime audit of all variable voicings across RBSA and BUSA. Quality gate before wiring tabtargets.

### Scope

For EVERY regime (BURC, BURS, BURD, RBRR, RBRN, RBRV, RBRP, RBRS, RBRA, RBRO):

**SKIP RBRE and RBRG — these regimes are being deleted elsewhere. Do NOT touch rbre_regime.sh, rbrg_regime.sh, RBSRE-RegimeEcr.adoc, RBSRG-RegimeGithub.adoc, or any RBRE/RBRG references.**

#### 1. Variable count match
Compare the number of variables in:
- The validator (regime.sh kindle function)
- The spec (RBSA/BUSA mapping section attribute references)
- The subdocument (axhrgv_variable / axhrv_variable markers)

All three counts must agree. Flag any discrepancies.

#### 2. Dimension consistency
For each variable, verify:
- `axd_required` / `axd_optional` / `axd_conditional` matches validator logic
- Type annotation (axtu_path, axtu_xname, axtu_string, etc.) matches buv_env_* call
- Conditional variables are in groups with proper axhrgc_gate markers

#### 3. Operation completeness
For each regime, verify:
- Singleton regimes have: kindle, validate, render
- Manifold regimes have: kindle, validate, render, list (minimum); survey/audit where implemented
- BURD: kindle and validate only (ephemeral, no render/list)
- All operation voicings use axhro_kindle (not axhro_broach — catch any missed renames)

#### 4. Cardinality annotation check
Every `// ⟦axvr_regime ...⟧` annotation includes axrd_singleton or axrd_manifold.

#### 5. Cross-document consistency
- Every RBSA/BUSA attribute reference has a corresponding anchor
- Every subdocument operation voices the correct kit-level term (rbkro_* or bukro_*)
- No orphaned attribute references (mapping entry with no anchor)

#### 6. Remove RBEV placeholder
RBEV is a dead concept. The RBSA section at ~line 2501 ("Environment Variables Regime") is a TBD placeholder with no variables, no validator, no CLI.

Actions:
- Remove the RBEV section from RBSA (the `=== Environment Variables Regime (RBEV)` block)
- Remove `rbev_regime` and `rbev_prefix` attribute references from the RBSA mapping section
- Remove corresponding anchors (`[[rbev_regime]]`, `[[rbev_prefix]]`)
- Verify no other documents reference the removed terms
- Note in audit memo: "RBEV was a placeholder concept; per-vessel build parameters are Dockerfile-local, not a regime"

### Output
Create a memo documenting findings: Memos/memo-20260216-regime-voicing-audit.md
List all fixes applied during this pace.

### Acceptance criteria
- Zero variable count discrepancies
- All dimensions match validator logic
- All operation markers use post-rename terms
- All cardinality annotations present
- RBEV placeholder removed from RBSA

## Warrant

Agent: opus | Cardinality: 1 sequential | Files: burc_regime.sh, burs_regime.sh, rbra_regime.sh, rbrn_regime.sh, rbro_regime.sh, rbrp_regime.sh, rbrr_regime.sh, rbrs_regime.sh, rbrv_regime.sh, RBSA-SpecTop.adoc, BUSA-BashUtilitiesSpec.adoc, BUSD-DispatchRuntime.adoc, RBSRR-RegimeRepo.adoc, RBSRV-RegimeVessel.adoc, RBRN-RegimeNameplate.adoc, RBSRP-RegimePayor.adoc, RBSRS-RegimeStation.adoc, RBSRA-CredentialFormat.adoc, RBSRO-RegimeOauth.adoc, memo-20260216-regime-voicing-audit.md (20 files) | Steps: 1. Read 9 regime.sh kindle functions, count variables per regime 2. Read RBSA and BUSA mapping sections, count attribute refs per regime 3. Read 7 subdocuments, count axhrgv_variable markers 4. Compare counts across all three sources, fix discrepancies 5. Verify dimension annotations match validator logic 6. Verify operation completeness -- singleton vs manifold sets 7. Check all cardinality annotations present 8. Cross-check attribute refs have anchors, no orphans 9. Remove RBEV placeholder section from RBSA 10. Write audit memo to Memos/memo-20260216-regime-voicing-audit.md | Verify: no axhro_broach in any .adoc | SKIP: Do NOT touch RBRE or RBRG -- these regimes are being deleted elsewhere

## File locations

Regime files:
- Tools/buk/burc_regime.sh
- Tools/buk/burs_regime.sh
- Tools/rbw/rbra_regime.sh
- Tools/rbw/rbrn_regime.sh
- Tools/rbw/rbro_regime.sh
- Tools/rbw/rbrp_regime.sh
- Tools/rbw/rbrr_regime.sh
- Tools/rbw/rbrs_regime.sh
- Tools/rbw/rbrv_regime.sh

Spec files:
- lenses/RBSA-SpecTop.adoc (main RBW spec)
- Tools/buk/vov_veiled/BUSA-BashUtilitiesSpec.adoc (BUK spec)

Subdocument files:
- lenses/RBSRR-RegimeRepo.adoc
- lenses/RBSRV-RegimeVessel.adoc
- lenses/RBRN-RegimeNameplate.adoc
- lenses/RBSRP-RegimePayor.adoc
- lenses/RBSRS-RegimeStation.adoc
- lenses/RBSRA-CredentialFormat.adoc
- lenses/RBSRO-RegimeOauth.adoc

BURD spec:
- Tools/buk/vov_veiled/BUSD-DispatchRuntime.adoc

Output memo:
- Memos/memo-20260216-regime-voicing-audit.md

## Commit Discipline

Use `/jjc-pace-notch` style commits. To commit your work, run:
```bash
echo "COMMIT_MESSAGE" | ./tt/vvw-r.RunVVX.sh jjx_record ATAAp FILE1 FILE2 ... --intent "description"
```
where FILE1, FILE2 etc. are the specific files you changed. Include ALL changed files explicitly.

## Wrap Discipline

DO NOT run jjx_close or mark the pace complete. When work is done:
1. Verify no axhro_broach in any .adoc files
2. Report completion status and list of all fixes applied
3. STOP — the calling agent will confirm wrap with the user

    42→  BURC_LOG_LAST="${BURC_LOG_LAST:-}"
    43→  BURC_LOG_EXT="${BURC_LOG_EXT:-}"
    44→
    45→  # Detect unexpected BURC_ variables
    46→  local z_known="BURC_STATION_FILE BURC_TABTARGET_DIR BURC_TABTARGET_DELIMITER BURC_TOOLS_DIR BURC_PROJECT_ROOT BURC_MANAGED_KITS BURC_TEMP_ROOT_DIR BURC_OUTPUT_ROOT_DIR BURC_LOG_LAST BURC_LOG_EXT"
    47→  ZBURC_UNEXPECTED=()
    48→  local z_var
    49→  for z_var in $(compgen -v BURC_); do
    50→    case " ${z_known} " in
    51→      *" ${z_var} "*) : ;;
    52→      *) ZBURC_UNEXPECTED+=("${z_var}") ;;
    53→    esac
    54→  done
    55→
    56→  # Export variables needed by child processes (exec'd dispatch, workbenches)
    57→  export BURC_TABTARGET_DIR
    58→  export BURC_TOOLS_DIR
    59→
    60→  ZBURC_KINDLED=1
    61→}
    62→
    63→zburc_sentinel() {
    64→  test "${ZBURC_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module burc not kindled - call zburc_kindle first"
    65→}
    66→
    67→# Validate BURC variables via buv_env_* (dies on first error)
    68→# Prerequisite: kindle must have been called; buv_validation.sh must be sourced
    69→zburc_validate_fields() {
    70→  zburc_sentinel
    71→

    73→  if test ${#ZBURC_UNEXPECTED[@]} -gt 0; then

    79→  buv_env_string      BURC_TABTARGET_DIR         1    128
    80→  buv_env_string      BURC_TABTARGET_DELIMITER   1      1
    81→  buv_env_string      BURC_TOOLS_DIR             1    128
    82→  buv_env_string      BURC_PROJECT_ROOT          1    512
    83→  buv_env_string      BURC_MANAGED_KITS          1    512
    84→  buv_env_string      BURC_TEMP_ROOT_DIR         1    512
    85→  buv_env_string      BURC_OUTPUT_ROOT_DIR       1    512
    86→  buv_env_xname       BURC_LOG_LAST              1     64
    87→  buv_env_xname       BURC_LOG_EXT               1     16

    27→######################################################################
    28→# Internal Functions (zrbra_*)

    30→zrbra_kindle() {
    31→  test -z "${ZRBRA_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbra already kindled"
    32→
    33→  # Set defaults for all fields (validate enforces required-ness)
    34→  RBRA_CLIENT_EMAIL="${RBRA_CLIENT_EMAIL:-}"
    35→  RBRA_PRIVATE_KEY="${RBRA_PRIVATE_KEY:-}"
    36→  RBRA_PROJECT_ID="${RBRA_PROJECT_ID:-}"
    37→  RBRA_TOKEN_LIFETIME_SEC="${RBRA_TOKEN_LIFETIME_SEC:-}"
    38→
    39→  # Detect unexpected RBRA_ variables
    40→  local z_known="RBRA_CLIENT_EMAIL RBRA_PRIVATE_KEY RBRA_PROJECT_ID RBRA_TOKEN_LIFETIME_SEC"
    41→  ZRBRA_UNEXPECTED=()
    42→  local z_var
    43→  for z_var in $(compgen -v RBRA_); do
    44→    case " ${z_known} " in
    45→      *" ${z_var} "*) : ;;
    46→      *) ZRBRA_UNEXPECTED+=("${z_var}") ;;
    47→    esac
    48→  done
    49→
    50→  # Build rollup of all RBRA_ variables for passing to scripts/containers
    51→  # CRITICAL SECURITY: mask RBRA_PRIVATE_KEY
    52→  ZRBRA_ROLLUP=""
    53→  ZRBRA_ROLLUP+="RBRA_CLIENT_EMAIL='${RBRA_CLIENT_EMAIL}' "
    54→  ZRBRA_ROLLUP+="RBRA_PRIVATE_KEY='[REDACTED]' "
    55→  ZRBRA_ROLLUP+="RBRA_PROJECT_ID='${RBRA_PROJECT_ID}' "
    56→  ZRBRA_ROLLUP+="RBRA_TOKEN_LIFETIME_SEC='${RBRA_TOKEN_LIFETIME_SEC}'"
    57→
    58→  ZRBRA_KINDLED=1
    59→}
    60→
    61→zrbra_sentinel() {
    62→  test "${ZRBRA_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbra not kindled - call zrbra_kindle first"
    63→}
    64→
    65→# Validate RBRA variables via buv_env_* (dies on first error)
    66→# Prerequisite: kindle must have been called; buv_validation.sh must be sourced
    67→zrbra_validate_fields() {

    69→
    70→  # Die on unexpected variables
    71→  if test ${#ZRBRA_UNEXPECTED[@]} -gt 0; then
    72→    buc_die "Unexpected RBRA_ variables: ${ZRBRA_UNEXPECTED[*]}"
    73→  fi
    74→
    75→  # Core credential fields
    76→  buv_env_string RBRA_CLIENT_EMAIL 1 256
    77→  buv_env_string RBRA_PRIVATE_KEY 1 4096
    78→  buv_env_string RBRA_PROJECT_ID 1 64
    79→  buv_env_decimal RBRA_TOKEN_LIFETIME_SEC 300 3600
    80→
    81→  # Additional format validation: client email must match service account pattern
    82→  if ! printf '%s' "${RBRA_CLIENT_EMAIL}" | grep -qE '\.iam\.gserviceaccount\.com$'; then
    83→    buc_die "RBRA_CLIENT_EMAIL does not match service account pattern (*.iam.gserviceaccount.com): '${RBRA_CLIENT_EMAIL}'"
    84→  fi
    85→
    86→  # Additional format validation: private key must contain PEM key material
    87→  if ! printf '%s' "${RBRA_PRIVATE_KEY}" | grep -q 'BEGIN'; then
    88→    buc_die "RBRA_PRIVATE_KEY does not contain PEM key material"
    89→  fi
    90→}

    96→# Loading happens through RBRR references; CLI handles per-file operations
    97→

    85→  ZRBRN_ROLLUP+="RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_CIDRS='${RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_CIDRS}' "
    86→  ZRBRN_ROLLUP+="RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_DOMAINS='${RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_DOMAINS}' "
    87→  ZRBRN_ROLLUP+="RBRN_VOLUME_MOUNTS='${RBRN_VOLUME_MOUNTS}'"
    88→
    89→  # Build docker env args array for container injection
    90→  # Usage: docker run "${ZRBRN_DOCKER_ENV[@]}" ...
    91→  ZRBRN_DOCKER_ENV=()
    92→  ZRBRN_DOCKER_ENV+=("-e" "RBRN_MONIKER=${RBRN_MONIKER}")
    93→  ZRBRN_DOCKER_ENV+=("-e" "RBRN_DESCRIPTION=${RBRN_DESCRIPTION}")
    94→  ZRBRN_DOCKER_ENV+=("-e" "RBRN_RUNTIME=${RBRN_RUNTIME}")
    95→  ZRBRN_DOCKER_ENV+=("-e" "RBRN_SENTRY_VESSEL=${RBRN_SENTRY_VESSEL}")
    96→  ZRBRN_DOCKER_ENV+=("-e" "RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL=${RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL}")
    97→  ZRBRN_DOCKER_ENV+=("-e" "RBRN_SENTRY_CONSECRATION=${RBRN_SENTRY_CONSECRATION}")
    98→  ZRBRN_DOCKER_ENV+=("-e" "RBRN_BOTTLE_CONSECRATION=${RBRN_BOTTLE_CONSECRATION}")
    99→  ZRBRN_DOCKER_ENV+=("-e" "RBRN_ENTRY_MODE=${RBRN_ENTRY_MODE}")
   100→  ZRBRN_DOCKER_ENV+=("-e" "RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION=${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION}")
   101→  ZRBRN_DOCKER_ENV+=("-e" "RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE=${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE}")
   102→  ZRBRN_DOCKER_ENV+=("-e" "RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP=${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP}")
   103→  ZRBRN_DOCKER_ENV+=("-e" "RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK=${RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK}")
   104→  ZRBRN_DOCKER_ENV+=("-e" "RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP=${RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP}")
   105→  ZRBRN_DOCKER_ENV+=("-e" "RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP=${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP}")
   106→  ZRBRN_DOCKER_ENV+=("-e" "RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN=${RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN}")
   107→  ZRBRN_DOCKER_ENV+=("-e" "RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE=${RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE}")
   108→  ZRBRN_DOCKER_ENV+=("-e" "RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE=${RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE}")
   109→  ZRBRN_DOCKER_ENV+=("-e" "RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_CIDRS=${RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_CIDRS}")
   110→  ZRBRN_DOCKER_ENV+=("-e" "RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_DOMAINS=${RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_DOMAINS}")
   111→  ZRBRN_DOCKER_ENV+=("-e" "RBRN_VOLUME_MOUNTS=${RBRN_VOLUME_MOUNTS}")
   112→
   113→  ZRBRN_KINDLED=1
   114→}
   115→
   116→zrbrn_sentinel() {
   117→  test "${ZRBRN_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbrn not kindled - call zrbrn_kindle first"
   118→}
   119→
   120→# Validate RBRN variables via buv_env_* (dies on first error)
   121→# Prerequisite: kindle must have been called; buv_validation.sh must be sourced
   122→zrbrn_validate_fields() {
   123→  zrbrn_sentinel
   124→
   125→  # Die on unexpected variables
   126→  if test ${#ZRBRN_UNEXPECTED[@]} -gt 0; then
   127→    buc_die "Unexpected RBRN_ variables: ${ZRBRN_UNEXPECTED[*]}"
   128→  fi
   129→
   130→  # Core nameplate identification
   131→  buv_env_xname       RBRN_MONIKER                 2     12
   132→  buv_env_string      RBRN_DESCRIPTION             0    120
   133→  buv_env_string      RBRN_RUNTIME                 1     16
   134→
   135→  # Validate runtime is docker or podman
   136→  case "${RBRN_RUNTIME}" in
   137→    docker|podman) : ;;
   138→    *) buc_die "Invalid RBRN_RUNTIME: '${RBRN_RUNTIME}' (must be 'docker' or 'podman')" ;;
   139→  esac
   140→
   141→  # Container image configuration
   142→  buv_env_fqin        RBRN_SENTRY_VESSEL        1    128
   143→  buv_env_fqin        RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL        1    128
   144→  buv_env_fqin        RBRN_SENTRY_CONSECRATION        1    128
   145→  buv_env_fqin        RBRN_BOTTLE_CONSECRATION        1    128
   146→
   147→  # Entry point configuration
   148→  case "${RBRN_ENTRY_MODE}" in
   149→    disabled|enabled) : ;;
   150→    *) buc_die "Invalid RBRN_ENTRY_MODE: '${RBRN_ENTRY_MODE}' (must be 'disabled' or 'enabled')" ;;
   151→  esac
   152→
   153→  # Enclave network configuration
   154→  buv_env_ipv4        RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP
   155→  buv_env_decimal     RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK         8     30
   156→  buv_env_ipv4        RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP
   157→  buv_env_ipv4        RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP
   158→
   159→  # Verify IPs fall within declared subnet
   160→  zrbrn_ip_in_subnet RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP}" "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP}" "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK}"
   161→  zrbrn_ip_in_subnet RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP}" "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP}" "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK}"
   162→
   163→  # Uplink configuration
   164→  buv_env_port        RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN
   165→  case "${RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE}" in
   166→    disabled|global|allowlist) : ;;
   167→    *) buc_die "Invalid RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE: '${RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE}' (must be 'disabled', 'global', or 'allowlist')" ;;
   168→  esac
   169→  case "${RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE}" in
   170→    disabled|global|allowlist) : ;;
   171→    *) buc_die "Invalid RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE: '${RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE}' (must be 'disabled', 'global', or 'allowlist')" ;;
   172→  esac
   173→
   174→  # Conditional entry port validation (Required: When ENTRY_MODE=enabled)
   175→  if [[ $RBRN_ENTRY_MODE == enabled ]]; then
   176→    buv_env_port    RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION
   177→    buv_env_port    RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE
   178→
   179→    test ${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION} -lt ${RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN} || \
   180→      buc_die "RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION must be less than RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN"
   181→    test ${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE} -lt ${RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN} || \
   182→      buc_die "RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE must be less than RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN"
   183→  fi
   184→
   185→  # Conditional allowlist validation (Required: When *_MODE=allowlist)
   186→  if [[ ${RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE} == allowlist ]]; then
   187→    buv_env_list_cidr RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_CIDRS
   188→  fi
   189→  if [[ ${RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE} == allowlist ]]; then
   190→    buv_env_list_domain RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_DOMAINS
   191→  fi
   192→
   193→  # Volume mount configuration (Required: No)
   194→  buv_env_string      RBRN_VOLUME_MOUNTS           0    240
   195→}
   196→
   197→######################################################################
   198→# Public Functions (rbrn_*)
   199→
   200→# Load nameplate regime by moniker
   201→# Usage: rbrn_load_moniker <moniker>
   202→# Constructs path, verifies file exists, sources, kindles, and validates
   203→rbrn_load_moniker() {
   204→  local z_moniker="${1:-}"
   205→  test -n "${z_moniker}" || buc_die "rbrn_load_moniker: moniker argument required"
   206→
   207→  local z_nameplate_file="${RBCC_KIT_DIR}/${RBCC_RBRN_PREFIX}${z_moniker}${RBCC_RBRN_EXT}"
   208→  test -f "${z_nameplate_file}" || buc_die "Nameplate not found: ${z_nameplate_file}"
   209→
   210→  source "${z_nameplate_file}" || buc_die "Failed to source nameplate: ${z_nameplate_file}"
   211→  zrbrn_kindle
   212→  zrbrn_validate_fields
   213→}
   214→
   215→# Load nameplate regime by file path
   216→# Usage: rbrn_load_file <path>
   217→# Takes explicit file path, sources, kindles, and validates
   218→rbrn_load_file() {
   219→  local z_file="${1:-}"
   220→  test -n "${z_file}" || buc_die "rbrn_load_file: file argument required"
   221→  test -f "${z_file}" || buc_die "rbrn_load_file: file not found: ${z_file}"
   222→
   223→  source "${z_file}" || buc_die "Failed to source nameplate: ${z_file}"
   224→  zrbrn_kindle
   225→  zrbrn_validate_fields
   226→}
   227→
   228→# List available nameplate monikers
   229→# Usage: rbrn_list
   230→# Returns list of concrete nameplate monikers by globbing rbrn_*.env files
   231→rbrn_list() {
   232→  local z_nameplate_files=("${RBCC_KIT_DIR}/${RBCC_RBRN_PREFIX}"*"${RBCC_RBRN_EXT}")
   233→
   234→  for z_file in "${z_nameplate_files[@]}"; do
   235→    test -f "${z_file}" || continue
   236→    local z_basename="${z_file##*/}"
   237→    local z_moniker="${z_basename#${RBCC_RBRN_PREFIX}}"
   238→    z_moniker="${z_moniker%${RBCC_RBRN_EXT}}"
   239→    echo "${z_moniker}"
   240→  done
   241→}
   242→
   243→######################################################################
   244→# Cross-Nameplate Functions
   245→#
   246→# These iterate all nameplates by direct-sourcing .env files in
   247→# subshells to avoid kindle-once guard conflicts.
   248→#
   249→# rbrn_preflight: Requires RBCC kindled
   250→# rbrn_survey:    Requires RBCC, RBGC, RBGD kindled + RBRR loaded
   251→# rbrn_audit:     Requires RBCC, RBGC, RBGD kindled + RBRR loaded
   252→
   253→# Convert dotted-quad IPv4 to integer for subnet arithmetic
   254→zrbrn_ip_to_int() {
   255→  local z_a z_b z_c z_d
   256→  IFS='.' read -r z_a z_b z_c z_d <<< "$1"
   257→  echo $(( (z_a << 24) + (z_b << 16) + (z_c << 8) + z_d ))
   258→}
   259→
   260→# Validate that an IP falls within a subnet (dies if not)
   261→# Usage: zrbrn_ip_in_subnet LABEL IP BASE MASK
   262→zrbrn_ip_in_subnet() {
   263→  local z_label="$1" z_ip="$2" z_base="$3" z_mask="$4"
   264→  local z_ip_int=$(zrbrn_ip_to_int "${z_ip}")
   265→  local z_base_int=$(zrbrn_ip_to_int "${z_base}")
   266→  local z_net_mask=$(( (0xFFFFFFFF << (32 - z_mask)) & 0xFFFFFFFF ))
   267→  if [[ $(( z_ip_int & z_net_mask )) -ne $(( z_base_int & z_net_mask )) ]]; then
   268→    buc_die "${z_label}=${z_ip} is not within subnet ${z_base}/${z_mask}"
   269→  fi
   270→}
   271→
   272→# Cross-nameplate conflict validation (silent on success, dies on conflict)
   273→# Checks: port uniqueness, subnet non-overlap, enclave IP uniqueness
   274→rbrn_preflight() {
   275→  zrbcc_sentinel
   276→
   277→  # Collect structured data from all nameplates via subshell sourcing
   278→  local z_data
   279→  z_data=$(
   280→    for z_m in $(rbrn_list); do
   281→      z_f="${RBCC_KIT_DIR}/${RBCC_RBRN_PREFIX}${z_m}${RBCC_RBRN_EXT}"
   282→      (
   283→        source "${z_f}"
   284→        echo "${RBRN_MONIKER}|${RBRN_ENTRY_MODE}|${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION:-0}|${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE:-0}|${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP}|${RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK}|${RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP}|${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP}"
   285→      )
   286→    done
   287→  )
   288→
   289→  # Parallel arrays for conflict detection (bash 3.2 compatible)
   290→  local z_ws_port_keys=()
   291→  local z_ws_port_vals=()
   292→  local z_enc_port_keys=()
   293→  local z_enc_port_vals=()
   294→  local z_ip_keys=()
   295→  local z_ip_vals=()
   296→  local z_net_starts=()
   297→  local z_net_ends=()
   298→  local z_net_owners=()
   299→
   300→  local z_mon z_entry z_ws z_enc z_base z_mask z_sentry z_bottle
   301→  while IFS='|' read -r z_mon z_entry z_ws z_enc z_base z_mask z_sentry z_bottle; do
   302→    test -n "${z_mon}" || continue
   303→
   304→    # Workstation and enclave port uniqueness (enabled entries only)
   305→    if [[ "${z_entry}" == "enabled" ]]; then
   306→      local z_i
   307→      for z_i in "${!z_ws_port_keys[@]}"; do
   308→        if [[ "${z_ws_port_keys[$z_i]}" == "${z_ws}" ]]; then
   309→          buc_die "Port conflict: RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION=${z_ws} claimed by both ${z_ws_port_vals[$z_i]} and ${z_mon}"
   310→        fi
   311→      done
   312→      z_ws_port_keys+=("${z_ws}")
   313→      z_ws_port_vals+=("${z_mon}")
   314→
   315→      for z_i in "${!z_enc_port_keys[@]}"; do
   316→        if [[ "${z_enc_port_keys[$z_i]}" == "${z_enc}" ]]; then
   317→          buc_die "Port conflict: RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE=${z_enc} claimed by both ${z_enc_port_vals[$z_i]} and ${z_mon}"
   318→        fi
   319→      done
   320→      z_enc_port_keys+=("${z_enc}")
   321→      z_enc_port_vals+=("${z_mon}")
   322→    fi
   323→
   324→    # Enclave IP uniqueness (all sentry and bottle IPs across nameplates)
   325→    local z_j
   326→    for z_j in "${!z_ip_keys[@]}"; do
   327→      if [[ "${z_ip_keys[$z_j]}" == "${z_sentry}" ]]; then
   328→        buc_die "IP conflict: ${z_sentry} claimed by ${z_mon} (sentry) and ${z_ip_vals[$z_j]}"
   329→      fi
   330→    done
   331→    z_ip_keys+=("${z_sentry}")
   332→    z_ip_vals+=("${z_mon}:sentry")
   333→
   334→    for z_j in "${!z_ip_keys[@]}"; do
   335→      if [[ "${z_ip_keys[$z_j]}" == "${z_bottle}" ]]; then
   336→        buc_die "IP conflict: ${z_bottle} claimed by ${z_mon} (bottle) and ${z_ip_vals[$z_j]}"
   337→      fi
   338→    done
   339→    z_ip_keys+=("${z_bottle}")
   340→    z_ip_vals+=("${z_mon}:bottle")
   341→
   342→    # Subnet non-overlap
   343→    local z_net_int=$(zrbrn_ip_to_int "${z_base}")
   344→    local z_net_mask_bits=$(( (0xFFFFFFFF << (32 - z_mask)) & 0xFFFFFFFF ))
   345→    local z_net_addr=$(( z_net_int & z_net_mask_bits ))
   346→    local z_net_size=$(( 1 << (32 - z_mask) ))
   347→    local z_net_end=$(( z_net_addr + z_net_size - 1 ))
   348→
   349→    local z_k
   350→    for z_k in "${!z_net_starts[@]}"; do
   351→      if [[ ${z_net_addr} -le ${z_net_ends[$z_k]} ]] && [[ ${z_net_starts[$z_k]} -le ${z_net_end} ]]; then
   352→        buc_die "Subnet overlap: ${z_base}/${z_mask} (${z_mon}) overlaps with network of ${z_net_owners[$z_k]}"
   353→      fi
   354→    done
   355→    z_net_starts+=("${z_net_addr}")
   356→    z_net_ends+=("${z_net_end}")
   357→    z_net_owners+=("${z_mon}")
   358→
   359→  done <<< "${z_data}"
   360→}
   361→
   362→# Fleet info table — non-opinionated display of all nameplate configuration
   363→# Requires: RBCC, RBGC, RBGD kindled + RBRR loaded
   364→rbrn_survey() {
   365→  zrbcc_sentinel
   366→  zrbgc_sentinel
   367→  zrbgd_sentinel
   368→  zrbrr_sentinel
   369→
   370→  local z_gar_base="${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}/${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}/${RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY}"
   371→
   372→  echo ""
   373→  printf "%-10s %-8s %6s %6s  %-17s %-14s %-14s  %3s %3s\n" \
   374→    "Moniker" "Entry" "WS" "Enc" "Subnet" "Sentry IP" "Bottle IP" "Snt" "Btl"
   375→  printf "%-10s %-8s %6s %6s  %-17s %-14s %-14s  %3s %3s\n" \
   376→    "--------" "-----" "------" "------" "-----------------" "--------------" "--------------" "---" "---"
   377→
   378→  local z_moniker z_file
   379→  for z_moniker in $(rbrn_list); do
   380→    z_file="${RBCC_KIT_DIR}/${RBCC_RBRN_PREFIX}${z_moniker}${RBCC_RBRN_EXT}"
   381→    (
   382→      source "${z_file}"
   383→
   384→      local z_sentry_img="${z_gar_base}/${RBRN_SENTRY_VESSEL}:${RBRN_SENTRY_CONSECRATION}${RBGC_ARK_SUFFIX_IMAGE}"
   385→      local z_bottle_img="${z_gar_base}/${RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL}:${RBRN_BOTTLE_CONSECRATION}${RBGC_ARK_SUFFIX_IMAGE}"
   386→
   387→      local z_sentry_local="--"
   388→      local z_bottle_local="--"
   389→      if command -v "${RBRN_RUNTIME}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
   390→        ${RBRN_RUNTIME} image inspect "${z_sentry_img}" >/dev/null 2>&1 && z_sentry_local="ok"
   391→        ${RBRN_RUNTIME} image inspect "${z_bottle_img}" >/dev/null 2>&1 && z_bottle_local="ok"
   392→      else
   393→        z_sentry_local="??"
   394→        z_bottle_local="??"
   395→      fi
   396→
   397→      local z_ws_port="${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION:-}"
   398→      local z_enc_port="${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE:-}"
   399→      if [[ "${RBRN_ENTRY_MODE}" != "enabled" ]]; then
   400→        z_ws_port="-"
   401→        z_enc_port="-"
   402→      fi
   403→
   404→      printf "%-10s %-8s %6s %6s  %-17s %-14s %-14s  %3s %3s\n" \
   405→        "${RBRN_MONIKER}" "${RBRN_ENTRY_MODE}" "${z_ws_port}" "${z_enc_port}" \
   406→        "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP}/${RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK}" \
   407→        "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP}" "${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP}" \
   408→        "${z_sentry_local}" "${z_bottle_local}"
   409→    )
   410→  done
   411→  echo ""
   412→}
   413→
   414→# Audit — survey display then preflight validation
   415→rbrn_audit() {
   416→  rbrn_survey
   417→  rbrn_preflight
   418→  buc_step "Cross-nameplate audit passed"
   419→}

    21→set -euo pipefail
    22→
    23→# Multiple inclusion detection
    24→test -z "${ZRBRO_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbro multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
    25→ZRBRO_SOURCED=1
    26→
    27→######################################################################
    28→# Internal Functions (zrbro_*)
    29→
    30→zrbro_kindle() {
    31→  test -z "${ZRBRO_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbro already kindled"
    32→
    33→  # Set defaults for all fields (validate enforces required-ness)
    34→  RBRO_CLIENT_SECRET="${RBRO_CLIENT_SECRET:-}"
    35→  RBRO_REFRESH_TOKEN="${RBRO_REFRESH_TOKEN:-}"
    36→
    37→  # Detect unexpected RBRO_ variables
    38→  local z_known="RBRO_CLIENT_SECRET RBRO_REFRESH_TOKEN"
    39→  ZRBRO_UNEXPECTED=()
    40→  local z_var
    41→  for z_var in $(compgen -v RBRO_); do
    42→    case " ${z_known} " in
    43→      *" ${z_var} "*) : ;;
    44→      *) ZRBRO_UNEXPECTED+=("${z_var}") ;;
    45→    esac
    46→  done
    47→
    48→  # Build rollup of all RBRO_ variables for passing to scripts/containers
    49→  # CRITICAL SECURITY: mask both secret values
    50→  ZRBRO_ROLLUP=""
    51→  ZRBRO_ROLLUP+="RBRO_CLIENT_SECRET='[REDACTED]' "
    52→  ZRBRO_ROLLUP+="RBRO_REFRESH_TOKEN='[REDACTED]'"
    53→
    54→  ZRBRO_KINDLED=1
    55→}
    56→
    57→zrbro_sentinel() {
    58→  test "${ZRBRO_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbro not kindled - call zrbro_kindle first"
    59→}
    60→
    61→# Validate RBRO variables via buv_env_* (dies on first error)
    62→# Prerequisite: kindle must have been called; buv_validation.sh must be sourced
    63→zrbro_validate_fields() {
    64→  zrbro_sentinel
    65→
    66→  # Die on unexpected variables
    67→  if test ${#ZRBRO_UNEXPECTED[@]} -gt 0; then

    69→  fi
    70→
    71→  # Both credentials are required strings
    72→  buv_env_string RBRO_CLIENT_SECRET 1 512
    73→  buv_env_string RBRO_REFRESH_TOKEN 1 512
    74→}
    75→
    76→######################################################################
    77→# Public Functions (rbro_*)
    78→
    79→# Load RBRO from the canonical location (~/.rbw/rbro.env)
    80→rbro_load() {
    81→  local z_rbro_dir="${HOME}/.rbw"
    82→  local z_rbro_file="${z_rbro_dir}/rbro.env"
    83→
    84→  # Check directory and file exist
    85→  test -d "${z_rbro_dir}" || buc_die "RBRO directory missing (~/.rbw) - run rbgp_payor_install"

    33→  # Requires RBGC for pattern constants

    36→  # Precompute suggested project ID for error messages

    38→
    39→  # Validate RBRP_PAYOR_PROJECT_ID exists
    40→  if [ -z "${RBRP_PAYOR_PROJECT_ID:-}" ]; then
    41→    buc_warn "RBRP_PAYOR_PROJECT_ID is not set"
    42→    buc_step "To set a fresh value, run:"
    43→    buc_code "sed -i '' 's/^RBRP_PAYOR_PROJECT_ID=.*/RBRP_PAYOR_PROJECT_ID=${z_suggested_id}/' rbrp.env"
    44→    buc_die "Cannot proceed without RBRP_PAYOR_PROJECT_ID"
    45→  fi
    46→
    47→  # Validate RBRP_PAYOR_PROJECT_ID matches global naming pattern

    50→    buc_step "Expected pattern: ${RBGC_GLOBAL_PREFIX}-${RBGC_GLOBAL_TYPE_PAYOR}-YYMMDDHHMMSS"
    51→    buc_step "To set a fresh value, run the following and then git commit:"
    52→    buc_code "sed -i '' 's/^RBRP_PAYOR_PROJECT_ID=.*/RBRP_PAYOR_PROJECT_ID=${z_suggested_id}/' rbrp.env"
    53→    buc_die "Invalid RBRP_PAYOR_PROJECT_ID format"
    54→  fi
    55→
    56→  # Validate RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID format (optional during initial setup)
    57→  if [ -n "${RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID:-}" ]; then
    58→    if ! printf '%s' "${RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID}" | grep -qE '^[A-Z0-9]{6}-[A-Z0-9]{6}-[A-Z0-9]{6}$'; then

    61→      buc_die "Invalid RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID format"
    62→    fi
    63→  fi
    64→
    65→  # Validate RBRP_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID format (optional during initial setup)
    66→  if [ -n "${RBRP_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID:-}" ]; then
    67→    if ! printf '%s' "${RBRP_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID}" | grep -qE '\.apps\.googleusercontent\.com$'; then

    74→  ZRBRP_KINDLED=1
    75→}

    87→rbrp_load() {

    40→  RBRR_CHOSEN_PODMAN_VERSION="${RBRR_CHOSEN_PODMAN_VERSION:-}"
    41→  RBRR_CHOSEN_VMIMAGE_ORIGIN="${RBRR_CHOSEN_VMIMAGE_ORIGIN:-}"
    42→  RBRR_CHOSEN_IDENTITY="${RBRR_CHOSEN_IDENTITY:-}"

    44→  RBRR_GCP_REGION="${RBRR_GCP_REGION:-}"
    45→  RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY="${RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY:-}"
    46→  RBRR_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE="${RBRR_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE:-}"

    48→  RBRR_GCB_MIN_CONCURRENT_BUILDS="${RBRR_GCB_MIN_CONCURRENT_BUILDS:-}"
    49→  RBRR_GOVERNOR_RBRA_FILE="${RBRR_GOVERNOR_RBRA_FILE:-}"
    50→  RBRR_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE="${RBRR_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE:-}"
    51→  RBRR_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE="${RBRR_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE:-}"
    52→  RBRR_GCB_GCRANE_IMAGE_REF="${RBRR_GCB_GCRANE_IMAGE_REF:-}"
    53→  RBRR_GCB_ORAS_IMAGE_REF="${RBRR_GCB_ORAS_IMAGE_REF:-}"
    54→  RBRR_GCB_GCLOUD_IMAGE_REF="${RBRR_GCB_GCLOUD_IMAGE_REF:-}"
    55→  RBRR_GCB_DOCKER_IMAGE_REF="${RBRR_GCB_DOCKER_IMAGE_REF:-}"
    56→  RBRR_GCB_SKOPEO_IMAGE_REF="${RBRR_GCB_SKOPEO_IMAGE_REF:-}"
    57→  RBRR_GCB_ALPINE_IMAGE_REF="${RBRR_GCB_ALPINE_IMAGE_REF:-}"
    58→  RBRR_GCB_SYFT_IMAGE_REF="${RBRR_GCB_SYFT_IMAGE_REF:-}"
    59→  RBRR_GCB_BINFMT_IMAGE_REF="${RBRR_GCB_BINFMT_IMAGE_REF:-}"
    60→
    61→  # Detect unexpected RBRR_ variables
    62→  local z_known="RBRR_VESSEL_DIR RBRR_DNS_SERVER RBRR_IGNITE_MACHINE_NAME RBRR_DEPLOY_MACHINE_NAME RBRR_CRANE_TAR_GZ RBRR_MANIFEST_PLATFORMS RBRR_CHOSEN_PODMAN_VERSION RBRR_CHOSEN_VMIMAGE_ORIGIN RBRR_CHOSEN_IDENTITY RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID RBRR_GCP_REGION RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY RBRR_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE RBRR_GCB_TIMEOUT RBRR_GCB_MIN_CONCURRENT_BUILDS RBRR_GOVERNOR_RBRA_FILE RBRR_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE RBRR_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE RBRR_GCB_GCRANE_IMAGE_REF RBRR_GCB_ORAS_IMAGE_REF RBRR_GCB_GCLOUD_IMAGE_REF RBRR_GCB_DOCKER_IMAGE_REF RBRR_GCB_SKOPEO_IMAGE_REF RBRR_GCB_ALPINE_IMAGE_REF RBRR_GCB_SYFT_IMAGE_REF RBRR_GCB_BINFMT_IMAGE_REF"

    66→    case " ${z_known} " in
    67→      *" ${z_var} "*) : ;;
    68→      *) ZRBRR_UNEXPECTED+=("${z_var}") ;;
    69→    esac
    70→  done
    71→
    72→  # Build rollup of all RBRR_ variables for passing to scripts/containers
    73→  ZRBRR_ROLLUP=""
    74→  ZRBRR_ROLLUP+="RBRR_VESSEL_DIR='${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}' "
    75→  ZRBRR_ROLLUP+="RBRR_DNS_SERVER='${RBRR_DNS_SERVER}' "
    76→  ZRBRR_ROLLUP+="RBRR_IGNITE_MACHINE_NAME='${RBRR_IGNITE_MACHINE_NAME}' "
    77→  ZRBRR_ROLLUP+="RBRR_DEPLOY_MACHINE_NAME='${RBRR_DEPLOY_MACHINE_NAME}' "
    78→  ZRBRR_ROLLUP+="RBRR_CRANE_TAR_GZ='${RBRR_CRANE_TAR_GZ}' "
    79→  ZRBRR_ROLLUP+="RBRR_MANIFEST_PLATFORMS='${RBRR_MANIFEST_PLATFORMS}' "
    80→  ZRBRR_ROLLUP+="RBRR_CHOSEN_PODMAN_VERSION='${RBRR_CHOSEN_PODMAN_VERSION}' "
    81→  ZRBRR_ROLLUP+="RBRR_CHOSEN_VMIMAGE_ORIGIN='${RBRR_CHOSEN_VMIMAGE_ORIGIN}' "
    82→  ZRBRR_ROLLUP+="RBRR_CHOSEN_IDENTITY='${RBRR_CHOSEN_IDENTITY}' "
    83→  ZRBRR_ROLLUP+="RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID='${RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}' "
    84→  ZRBRR_ROLLUP+="RBRR_GCP_REGION='${RBRR_GCP_REGION}' "
    85→  ZRBRR_ROLLUP+="RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY='${RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY}' "
    86→  ZRBRR_ROLLUP+="RBRR_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE='${RBRR_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE}' "
    87→  ZRBRR_ROLLUP+="RBRR_GCB_TIMEOUT='${RBRR_GCB_TIMEOUT}' "
    88→  ZRBRR_ROLLUP+="RBRR_GOVERNOR_RBRA_FILE='${RBRR_GOVERNOR_RBRA_FILE}' "
    89→  ZRBRR_ROLLUP+="RBRR_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE='${RBRR_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}' "
    90→  ZRBRR_ROLLUP+="RBRR_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE='${RBRR_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}' "
    91→  ZRBRR_ROLLUP+="RBRR_GCB_GCRANE_IMAGE_REF='${RBRR_GCB_GCRANE_IMAGE_REF}' "
    92→  ZRBRR_ROLLUP+="RBRR_GCB_ORAS_IMAGE_REF='${RBRR_GCB_ORAS_IMAGE_REF}' "

    97→  ZRBRR_ROLLUP+="RBRR_GCB_SYFT_IMAGE_REF='${RBRR_GCB_SYFT_IMAGE_REF}' "
    98→  ZRBRR_ROLLUP+="RBRR_GCB_BINFMT_IMAGE_REF='${RBRR_GCB_BINFMT_IMAGE_REF}'"
    99→
   100→  # Build docker env args array for container injection
   101→  # Usage: docker run "${ZRBRR_DOCKER_ENV[@]}" ...
   102→  # Currently only RBRR_DNS_SERVER is needed by sentry; add others as needed
   103→  ZRBRR_DOCKER_ENV=()

   109→zrbrr_sentinel() {
   110→  test "${ZRBRR_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbrr not kindled - call zrbrr_kindle first"
   111→}
   112→
   113→# Validate RBRR variables via buv_env_* (dies on first error)
   114→# Prerequisite: kindle must have been called; buv_validation.sh must be sourced
   115→zrbrr_validate_fields() {
   116→  zrbrr_sentinel
   117→
   118→  # Die on unexpected variables
   119→  if test ${#ZRBRR_UNEXPECTED[@]} -gt 0; then
   120→    buc_die "Unexpected RBRR_ variables: ${ZRBRR_UNEXPECTED[*]}"
   121→  fi
   122→
   123→  # Container Registry Configuration
   124→  buv_env_string      RBRR_VESSEL_DIR              1    255
   125→  buv_env_ipv4        RBRR_DNS_SERVER
   126→
   127→  # Machine Configuration
   128→  buv_env_xname       RBRR_IGNITE_MACHINE_NAME     1     64
   129→  buv_env_xname       RBRR_DEPLOY_MACHINE_NAME     1     64
   130→  buv_env_string      RBRR_CRANE_TAR_GZ            1    512
   131→  buv_env_string      RBRR_MANIFEST_PLATFORMS      1    512
   132→  buv_env_string      RBRR_CHOSEN_PODMAN_VERSION   1     16
   133→  buv_env_fqin        RBRR_CHOSEN_VMIMAGE_ORIGIN   1    256
   134→  buv_env_string      RBRR_CHOSEN_IDENTITY         1    128
   135→  buv_env_gname       RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID          6     63
   136→  buv_env_gname       RBRR_GCP_REGION              1     32
   137→
   138→  # Google Artifact Registry Configuration
   139→  buv_env_gname       RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY          1     63
   140→
   141→  # Google Cloud Build Configuration
   142→  buv_env_string      RBRR_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE        3     64
   143→  buv_env_string      RBRR_GCB_TIMEOUT             2     10
   144→
   145→  # Service Account Configuration Files
   146→  buv_env_string      RBRR_GOVERNOR_RBRA_FILE         1    512
   147→  buv_env_string      RBRR_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE     1    512
   148→  buv_env_string      RBRR_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE      1    512
   149→
   150→  # GCB image pins (digest-pinned)
   151→  buv_env_odref       RBRR_GCB_GCRANE_IMAGE_REF
   152→  buv_env_odref       RBRR_GCB_ORAS_IMAGE_REF
   153→  buv_env_odref       RBRR_GCB_GCLOUD_IMAGE_REF
   154→  buv_env_odref       RBRR_GCB_DOCKER_IMAGE_REF
   155→  buv_env_odref       RBRR_GCB_SKOPEO_IMAGE_REF
   156→  buv_env_odref       RBRR_GCB_ALPINE_IMAGE_REF
   157→  buv_env_odref       RBRR_GCB_SYFT_IMAGE_REF
   158→  buv_env_odref       RBRR_GCB_BINFMT_IMAGE_REF
   159→
   160→  # Validate directories exist
   161→  buv_dir_exists "${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}"
   162→
   163→  # Validate manifest platforms format (space-separated identifiers)
   164→  local z_platform=""
   165→  for z_platform in ${RBRR_MANIFEST_PLATFORMS}; do
   166→    if ! printf '%s' "${z_platform}" | grep -q '^[a-z0-9_]\+$'; then
   167→      buc_warn "Invalid platform format in RBRR_MANIFEST_PLATFORMS: ${z_platform}"
   168→      buc_step "Expected format: lowercase alphanumeric with underscores"
   169→      buc_die "Invalid RBRR_MANIFEST_PLATFORMS"
   170→    fi
   171→  done
   172→
   173→  # Validate timeout format (number followed by 's' for seconds)
   174→  if ! printf '%s' "${RBRR_GCB_TIMEOUT}" | grep -q '^[0-9]\+s$'; then
   175→    buc_warn "Invalid RBRR_GCB_TIMEOUT format: ${RBRR_GCB_TIMEOUT}"
   176→    buc_step "Expected format: number followed by 's' (e.g., 1200s)"
   177→    buc_die "Invalid RBRR_GCB_TIMEOUT"
   178→  fi
   179→
   180→  # Validate Podman version format (e.g., 5.5 or 5.5.1)
   181→  if ! printf '%s' "${RBRR_CHOSEN_PODMAN_VERSION}" | grep -q '^[0-9]\+\.[0-9]\+\(\.[0-9]\+\)\?$'; then
   182→    buc_warn "Invalid RBRR_CHOSEN_PODMAN_VERSION format: ${RBRR_CHOSEN_PODMAN_VERSION}"
   183→    buc_step "Expected format: semantic version like 5.5 or 5.5.1"
   184→    buc_die "Invalid RBRR_CHOSEN_PODMAN_VERSION"
   185→  fi
   186→}
   187→
   188→######################################################################
   189→# Public Functions (rbrr_*)
   190→
   191→# Load RBRR regime
   192→# Usage: rbrr_load
   193→# Uses RBCC_RBRR_FILE to locate rbrr.env, verifies exists, sources, kindles, and validates
   194→rbrr_load() {
   195→  local z_rbrr_file="${RBCC_RBRR_FILE}"
   196→  test -f "${z_rbrr_file}" || buc_die "RBRR config not found: ${z_rbrr_file}"
   197→
   198→  source "${z_rbrr_file}" || buc_die "Failed to source RBRR config: ${z_rbrr_file}"
   199→  zrbrr_kindle
   200→  zrbrr_validate_fields
   201→}

    21→set -euo pipefail
    22→
    23→# Multiple inclusion detection
    24→test -z "${ZRBRV_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbrv multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
    25→ZRBRV_SOURCED=1
    26→
    27→######################################################################
    28→# Internal Functions (zrbrv_*)
    29→
    30→zrbrv_kindle() {
    31→  test -z "${ZRBRV_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbrv already kindled"
    32→
    33→  # Set defaults for all fields (validate enforces required-ness)
    34→  RBRV_SIGIL="${RBRV_SIGIL:-}"
    35→  RBRV_DESCRIPTION="${RBRV_DESCRIPTION:-}"
    36→  RBRV_BIND_IMAGE="${RBRV_BIND_IMAGE:-}"
    37→  RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE="${RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE:-}"
    38→  RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT="${RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT:-}"
    39→  RBRV_CONJURE_PLATFORMS="${RBRV_CONJURE_PLATFORMS:-}"
    40→  RBRV_CONJURE_BINFMT_POLICY="${RBRV_CONJURE_BINFMT_POLICY:-}"
    41→
    42→  # Detect unexpected RBRV_ variables
    43→  local z_known="RBRV_SIGIL RBRV_DESCRIPTION RBRV_BIND_IMAGE RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT RBRV_CONJURE_PLATFORMS RBRV_CONJURE_BINFMT_POLICY"
    44→  ZRBRV_UNEXPECTED=()
    45→  local z_var
    46→  for z_var in $(compgen -v RBRV_); do
    47→    case " ${z_known} " in
    48→      *" ${z_var} "*) : ;;
    49→      *) ZRBRV_UNEXPECTED+=("${z_var}") ;;
    50→    esac
    51→  done
    52→
    53→  # Build rollup of all RBRV_ variables for passing to scripts/containers
    54→  ZRBRV_ROLLUP=""
    55→  ZRBRV_ROLLUP+="RBRV_SIGIL='${RBRV_SIGIL}' "
    56→  ZRBRV_ROLLUP+="RBRV_DESCRIPTION='${RBRV_DESCRIPTION}' "
    57→  ZRBRV_ROLLUP+="RBRV_BIND_IMAGE='${RBRV_BIND_IMAGE}' "
    58→  ZRBRV_ROLLUP+="RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE='${RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE}' "
    59→  ZRBRV_ROLLUP+="RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT='${RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT}' "
    60→  ZRBRV_ROLLUP+="RBRV_CONJURE_PLATFORMS='${RBRV_CONJURE_PLATFORMS}' "
    61→  ZRBRV_ROLLUP+="RBRV_CONJURE_BINFMT_POLICY='${RBRV_CONJURE_BINFMT_POLICY}'"
    62→
    63→  ZRBRV_KINDLED=1
    64→}
    65→
    66→zrbrv_sentinel() {
    67→  test "${ZRBRV_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbrv not kindled - call zrbrv_kindle first"
    68→}
    69→
    70→# Validate RBRV variables via buv_env_* (dies on first error)
    71→# Prerequisite: kindle must have been called; buv_validation.sh must be sourced
    72→zrbrv_validate_fields() {
    73→  zrbrv_sentinel
    74→
    75→  # Die on unexpected variables
    76→  if test ${#ZRBRV_UNEXPECTED[@]} -gt 0; then
    77→    buc_die "Unexpected RBRV_ variables: ${ZRBRV_UNEXPECTED[*]}"
    78→  fi
    79→
    80→  # Core Vessel Identity
    81→  buv_env_xname       RBRV_SIGIL                   1     64  # Must match directory name
    82→  buv_env_string      RBRV_DESCRIPTION             0    512  # Optional description
    83→
    84→  # Binding Configuration (for copying from registry)
    85→  if test -n "${RBRV_BIND_IMAGE}"; then
    86→    buv_env_fqin      RBRV_BIND_IMAGE              1    512  # Source image to copy
    87→  fi
    88→
    89→  # Conjuring Configuration (for building from source)
    90→  if test -n "${RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE}"; then
    91→    buv_env_string    RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE      1    512  # Path relative to repo root
    92→    buv_env_string    RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT      1    512  # Build context relative to repo root
    93→    buv_env_string    RBRV_CONJURE_PLATFORMS       1    512  # Space-separated platforms
    94→    buv_env_string    RBRV_CONJURE_BINFMT_POLICY   1     16  # Either "allow" or "forbid"
    95→    case "${RBRV_CONJURE_BINFMT_POLICY}" in
    96→      allow|forbid) : ;;
    97→      *) buc_die "Invalid RBRV_CONJURE_BINFMT_POLICY: '${RBRV_CONJURE_BINFMT_POLICY}' (must be 'allow' or 'forbid')" ;;
    98→    esac
    99→  fi
   100→
   101→  # Validate at least one operation mode is configured
   102→  if test -z "${RBRV_BIND_IMAGE}" && test -z "${RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE}"; then
   103→    buc_die "Vessel must define either RBRV_BIND_IMAGE (for binding) or RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE (for conjuring)"
   104→  fi
   105→}
   106→
   107→######################################################################
   108→# Public Functions (rbrv_*)
   109→
   110→# List available vessel sigils
   111→# Usage: rbrv_list
   112→# Prerequisite: RBRR must be loaded (needs RBRR_VESSEL_DIR)
   113→rbrv_list() {
   114→  zrbrr_sentinel
   115→  for z_d in "${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}"/*/; do
   116→    test -d "${z_d}" || continue
   117→    test -f "${z_d}/rbrv.env" || continue
   118→    local z_s="${z_d%/}"
   119→    echo "${z_s##*/}"
   120→  done
   121→}
   122→
   123→# eof
   124→

   440→// TIER 1: WHY AND WHAT
   441→// ============================================================================
   442→
   443→== System Overview
   444→
   445→=== Core Trust Challenge
   446→
   447→Modern development increasingly requires running foreign code with access to local work.
   448→{st_container}-based development tools processing source code, local AI/ML tools analyzing proprietary data, and third-party services needing controlled network access create fundamental security challenges.
   449→
   450→This creates a fundamental trust dilemma:
   451→
   452→*
   453→{st_container_s}
   454→provide powerful tool isolation
   455→* But they need controlled access to do useful work
   456→* Standard
   457→{st_container}
   458→networking grants unfettered outbound access or else network services completely unavailable.
   459→
   460→=== The Bottle Pattern
   461→
   462→{at_rbm_system}
   463→solves this by creating two simple, robust boundaries around a certain
   464→{st_container}
   465→type here referred to as a
   466→{at_bottle_container}:
   467→
   468→. File Access Boundary
   469→*
   470→{at_bottle_container}
   471→sees only explicitly controlled set of
   472→{st_volume_mount_s}
   473→* No other access to
   474→{at_workstation}
   475→filesystem
   476→
   477→. Network Boundary
   478→*
   479→{at_bottle_container}
   480→has no direct network access
   481→* All traffic flows through dedicated
   482→{at_sentry_container},
   483→a
   484→{st_container}
   485→configured with very tight network access and visibility controls.
   486→* Only declared
   487→{st_port_map_s}
   488→and destinations allowed
   489→* Full logging of allowed and denied traffic
   490→
   491→Each
   492→{at_bottle_service}
   493→maintains these boundaries through a three-container architecture:
   494→
   495→* One
   496→{at_sentry_container}
   497→enforcing network policy on dual networks (bridge and enclave)
   498→* One
   499→{at_censer_container}
   500→establishing and configuring the network namespace

   118→
   119→== Overview
   120→
   121→The Bash Utilities Specification defines the tabtarget dispatch system — the infrastructure for tab-completion-friendly launchers that route to kit formularies.
   122→
   123→BUK invented tabtargets; BUK defines them.
   124→
   125→For universal naming vocabulary (Cipher, Signet, Epithet, Inscription, Vesture), see VLS-VoxLiturgicalSpec.adoc.
   126→
   127→== Tabtarget Vesture
   128→
   129→[[bust_tabtarget]]
   130→// ⟦axl_voices axo_entity⟧
   131→{bust_tabtarget}::
   132→Vesture for tabtarget launcher identifiers.
   133→Defines how launcher scripts in `tt/` are named.
   134→+
   135→* signet_case: lowercase with hyphen (the colophon)
   136→* separator: `.`
   137→* epithet_case: PascalCase (the frontispiece)
   138→* envelope: `.sh`
   139→+
   140→Pattern: `{colophon}.{frontispiece}[.{imprint}].sh`
   141→+
   142→Examples:
   143→+
   144→* `rbw-B.ConnectBottle.sh` — RBW workbench, ConnectBottle operation
   145→* `rbw-B.ConnectBottle.nsproto.sh` — same, with imprint specifying target
   146→* `jjw-.HeatMount.sh` — JJK workbench, HeatMount operation
   147→* `buw-tt.LauncherList.sh` — BUK tabtarget testbench, LauncherList operation
   148→
   149→== Dispatch
   150→
   151→The dispatch vocabulary defines the tabtarget launcher system.
   152→A
   153→{busdl_launcher}
   154→validates arguments and delegates to a
   155→{busdm_formulary}.
   156→The
   157→{busdc_colophon}
   158→identifies the routing path;
   159→the
   160→{busdf_frontispiece}
   161→provides human-readable description;
   162→the
   163→{busdi_imprint}
   164→embeds target parameters.
   165→
   166→[[busdc_colophon]]
   167→// ⟦axl_voices axt_string⟧
   168→{busdc_colophon}::
   169→The routing identifier in a tabtarget inscription.
   170→A
   171→{busdc_colophon}
   172→determines which
   173→{busdm_formulary}
   174→handles the dispatch.
   175→Includes the hyphen as part of its identity (e.g., `rbw-`, `buw-tt-`).
   176→+
   177→The
   178→{busdc_colophon}
   179→is the first component of the tabtarget pattern, before the first dot separator.
   180→+
   181→Examples:
   182→+
   183→* `rbw-` routes to RBW workbench
   184→* `jjw-` routes to JJK workbench
   185→* `buw-tt-` routes to BUK tabtarget testbench
   186→
   187→[[busdf_frontispiece]]
   188→// ⟦axl_voices axt_string⟧
   189→{busdf_frontispiece}::
   190→The human-readable description in a tabtarget inscription.
   191→A
   192→{busdf_frontispiece}
   193→uses PascalCase and describes the operation.
   194→+
   195→Appears after the
   196→{busdc_colophon}
   197→and dot separator.
   198→+
   199→Examples: `Build`, `ConnectBottle`, `RunVVX`, `HeatMount`.
   200→
   201→[[busdi_imprint]]
   202→// ⟦axl_voices axt_string⟧
   203→{busdi_imprint}::
   204→An optional embedded parameter in a tabtarget inscription.
   205→An
   206→{busdi_imprint}
   207→specifies a target, instance, or configuration variant.
   208→+
   209→Appears after a second dot separator following the
   210→{busdf_frontispiece}.
   211→+
   212→Examples:
   213→+
   214→* `nsproto` in `rbw-B.ConnectBottle.nsproto.sh`
   215→* `srjcl` in `rbw-B.ConnectBottle.srjcl.sh`
   216→
   217→[[busdm_formulary]]
   218→// ⟦axl_voices axo_entity⟧
   219→{busdm_formulary}::
   220→A component that routes
   221→{busdc_colophon_s}
   222→to implementations.
   223→Each
   224→{busdm_formulary}
   225→registers handlers for a set of
   226→{busdc_colophon_s}
   227→and dispatches to the appropriate function.
   228→+
   229→Types:
   230→+
   231→* **Workbench** (`{prefix}w_workbench.sh`): Routes commands for a kit
   232→* **Testbench** (`{prefix}t_testbench.sh`): Routes tests for a kit
   233→+
   234→A
   235→{busdm_formulary}
   236→is sourced by the
   237→{busdl_launcher}
   238→and provides the dispatch function.
   239→
   240→[[busdl_launcher]]
   241→// ⟦axl_voices axo_entity⟧
   242→{busdl_launcher}::
   243→A bootstrap script in the `tt/` directory that validates arguments and delegates to a
   244→{busdm_formulary}.
   245→A
   246→{busdl_launcher}
   247→is the entry point for tabtarget invocation.
   248→+
   249→Naming follows
   250→{bust_tabtarget}:
   251→`{colophon}.{frontispiece}[.{imprint}].sh`
   252→+
   253→Responsibilities:
   254→+
   255→* Parse
   256→{busdc_colophon}
   257→from own filename
   258→* Validate required arguments
   259→* Source the target
   260→{busdm_formulary}
   261→* Invoke the dispatched function
   262→
   263→== Routing Behavior
   264→
   265→=== Dispatch Flow
   266→
   267→. User invokes launcher: `./tt/rbw-B.ConnectBottle.nsproto.sh`
   268→. Launcher parses its filename to extract:
   269→* {busdc_colophon}:
   270→`rbw-B`
   271→* {busdf_frontispiece}:
   272→`ConnectBottle`
   273→* {busdi_imprint}:
   274→`nsproto`
   275→. Launcher sources the appropriate
   276→{busdm_formulary}
   277→(`rbw_workbench.sh`)
   278→. Formulary's dispatch function routes based on frontispiece
   279→. Target function executes with imprint as parameter
   280→
   281→=== Workbench Pattern
   282→
   283→[source,bash]
   284→----
   285→# Tools/{kit}/{prefix}w_workbench.sh
   286→
   287→{prefix}w_dispatch() {
   288→    local frontispiece="$1"
   289→    local imprint="$2"
   290→    shift 2
   291→
   292→    case "$frontispiece" in
   293→        Build)          {prefix}w_build "$imprint" "$@" ;;
   294→        ConnectBottle)  {prefix}w_connect_bottle "$imprint" "$@" ;;
   295→        *)              buc_fatal "Unknown frontispiece: $frontispiece" ;;
   296→    esac
   297→}
   298→----
   299→
   300→=== Testbench Pattern
   301→
   302→Testbenches follow the same pattern but route to test functions:
   303→
   304→[source,bash]
   305→----
   306→# Tools/{kit}/{prefix}t_testbench.sh
   307→
   308→{prefix}t_dispatch() {
   309→    local frontispiece="$1"
   310→    shift
   311→
   312→    case "$frontispiece" in
   313→        Unit)        {prefix}t_unit "$@" ;;
   314→        Integration) {prefix}t_integration "$@" ;;
   315→        *)           buc_fatal "Unknown test: $frontispiece" ;;
   316→    esac
   317→}
   318→----
   319→
   320→=== Colophon Conventions
   321→
   322→{busdc_colophon_s}
   323→follow a structure that encodes both kit identity and formulary type:
   324→
   325→* `{cipher}-` — default workbench (e.g., `rbw-`, `jjw-`)
   326→* `{cipher}-t-` — testbench (e.g., `buw-t-`, `jjw-t-`)
   327→* `{cipher}-{variant}-` — specialized workbench (e.g., `rbw-B-` for bottle operations)
   328→
   329→The trailing hyphen is always part of the colophon — it separates the routing prefix from the dot-delimited tabtarget components.
   330→

   455→=== BURD Regime
   456→
   457→[[burd_prefix]]
   458→// ⟦axl_voices axrg_prefix⟧

   463→// ⟦axvr_regime axf_bash axrd_constructed axrd_singleton⟧

1→// ⟦axhrb_regime⟧
     2→{rbrr_regime}
     3→
     4→This regime specifies variables that define repository-wide configuration and installation-wide settings within the Recipe Bottle system.
     5→Each
     6→{rbrr_regime}
     7→instance represents foundational infrastructure configuration that rarely changes and is shared across all Recipe Bottle operations.
     8→RBRR assignment files are found in the operator's local filesystem (typically `../station-files/rbrr.env`) and configure depot project identifiers, regional settings, repository locations, and service account credential file paths.
     9→
    10→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧
    11→{rbrr_depot_project_id}
    12→
    13→The
    14→{gcp_service}
    15→{gcp_project_id}
    16→where all Recipe Bottle resources are created and managed.
    17→Used as the target
    18→{gcp_project_id}
    19→in API calls and as the audience claim in OAuth token exchange.
    20→
    21→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧
    22→{rbrr_gcp_region}
    23→
    24→The default
    25→{gcp_service}
    26→region for regional services like
    27→{gar_service}
    28→and
    29→{gcb_service}.
    30→Applied when creating repositories and submitting builds unless overridden by operation-specific configuration.
    31→
    32→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧
    33→{rbrr_gar_repository}
    34→
    35→The
    36→{gar_service}
    37→repository name within
    38→{rbrr_depot_project_id}
    39→that stores all bottle images and metadata.
    40→
    41→// ⟦axhrgb_group⟧
    42→{rbrr_group_service_accounts}
    43→
    44→Service account credential file paths that enable different operational roles.
    45→Each RBRA file contains the minimum identity and signing material needed for authentication to
    46→{gcp_service}
    47→via service account.
    48→
    49→// ⟦axhrgv_variable⟧
    50→{rbrr_retriever_rbra_file}
    51→
    52→Path to an RBRA file stored in the operator's local filesystem outside the source repository.
    53→This file enables the operator to assume the retriever role for container image operations.
    54→
    55→// ⟦axhrgv_variable⟧
    56→{rbrr_director_rbra_file}
    57→
    58→Path to an RBRA file stored in the operator's local filesystem outside the source repository.
    59→This file enables the operator to assume the director role for build submission and image management operations.
    60→
    61→// ⟦axhrgv_variable⟧
    62→{rbrr_governor_rbra_file}
    63→
    64→Path to an RBRA file stored in the operator's local filesystem outside the source repository.
    65→This file enables the operator to assume the governor role for
    66→{gcp_project_id}
    67→administration operations.
    68→
    69→// ⟦axhrgv_variable⟧
    70→{rbrr_payor_rbra_file}
    71→
    72→Path to an RBRA file stored in the operator's local filesystem outside the source repository.
    73→This file enables the operator to assume the payor role for billing and
    74→{gcp_project_id}
    75→lifecycle operations.
    76→
    77→// ⟦axhrgb_group⟧
    78→{rbrr_group_container_runtime}
    79→
    80→Variables that configure container runtime and deployment environment settings.
    81→These settings control local Podman VM configuration, DNS resolution, and build machine selection.
    82→
    83→// ⟦axhrgv_variable⟧
    84→{rbrr_vessel_dir}
    85→
    86→Directory path containing vessel configuration subdirectories.
    87→Each subdirectory defines a vessel image source (bind or conjure mode).
    88→
    89→// ⟦axhrgv_variable⟧
    90→{rbrr_dns_server}
    91→
    92→DNS server IP address used by sentry containers for name resolution.
    93→Injected into container environment to control DNS behavior.
    94→
    95→// ⟦axhrgv_variable⟧
    96→{rbrr_ignite_machine_name}
    97→
    98→Podman machine name for bottle ignition (startup) operations.
    99→Identifies which Podman VM to use for local image building and testing.
   100→
   101→// ⟦axhrgv_variable⟧
   102→{rbrr_deploy_machine_name}
   103→
   104→Podman machine name for bottle deployment operations.
   105→Identifies which Podman VM to use for production deployments.
   106→
   107→// ⟦axhrgb_group⟧
   108→{rbrr_group_build_configuration}
   109→
   110→Variables that control container image build processes.
   111→These settings define build tooling, platform targets, timeout constraints, and machine resources for
   112→{gcb_service}
   113→operations.
   114→
   115→// ⟦axhrgv_variable⟧
   116→{rbrr_crane_tar_gz}
   117→
   118→Path to crane tool tarball archive.

   120→
   121→// ⟦axhrgv_variable⟧
   122→{rbrr_manifest_platforms}
   123→
   124→Space-separated list of platforms for multi-platform manifest creation.

   127→// ⟦axhrgv_variable⟧
   128→{rbrr_chosen_podman_version}
   129→
   130→Podman version string for VM provisioning and build operations.

   133→// ⟦axhrgv_variable⟧

   139→// ⟦axhrgv_variable⟧

   145→// ⟦axhrgv_variable⟧

   147→
   148→Google Cloud Build machine type specification.

   152→// ⟦axhrgv_variable⟧
   153→{rbrr_gcb_min_concurrent_builds}
   154→
   155→Minimum number of concurrent Cloud Build executions required.
   156→The nameplate audit and build preflight query the depot project's *Concurrent Build CPUs* quota via the Service Usage API and verify that `CPU_QUOTA ÷ vCPUs_per_build ≥` this value.
   157→Hard-fails if quota headroom is insufficient.

   159→// ⟦axhrgv_variable⟧
   160→{rbrr_gcb_timeout}
   161→
   162→Maximum duration for Cloud Build operations before timeout.

   165→// ⟦axhrgv_variable⟧
   166→{rbrr_gcb_gcrane_image_ref}
   167→
   168→Fully-qualified image reference for gcrane tool used in Cloud Build steps.

   171→// ⟦axhrgv_variable⟧
   172→{rbrr_gcb_oras_image_ref}

   174→Fully-qualified image reference for ORAS tool used in Cloud Build steps.
   175→ORAS handles OCI artifact operations beyond container images.

   177→// ⟦axhro_kindle⟧
   178→{rbkro_kindle}
   179→
   180→Sets defaults for all RBRR_ variables via `VAR="${VAR:-}"` pattern.
   181→Detects unexpected RBRR_ variables via compgen prefix scan.
   182→Builds ZRBRR_ROLLUP serialization string and ZRBRR_DOCKER_ENV injection array.

   184→// ⟦axhro_validate⟧

   190→Validates machine type and timeout format constraints.

   192→// ⟦axhro_render⟧
   193→{rbkro_render}
   194→
   195→Renders via rbcr_* shared module.
   196→Sections: GCP Infrastructure, Artifact Registry, Service Account Credentials, Container Runtime Configuration, Build Configuration.
   197→Displays unexpected variables in red.
   198→Validates after full render completes.

1→// ⟦axhrb_regime⟧

     3→
     4→This regime specifies variables that define container vessel configurations within the Recipe Bottle system.
     5→Each
     6→{rbrv_regime}

    35→// ⟦axhrgc_gate⟧
    36→{rbrv_vessel_mode}

1→// ⟦axhrb_regime⟧
     2→{rbrn_regime}
     3→
     4→This regime specifies variables that define bottle service deployment configurations within the Recipe Bottle system.
     5→Each
     6→{rbrn_regime}
     7→instance represents a distinct service deployment with its own network, entry, and uplink settings.

    10→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧

    13→Unique moniker for the bottle service instance.

    15→
    16→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧
    17→{rbrn_description}

    19→Human-readable description of the service purpose.
    20→Optional; 0-120 characters.
    21→
    22→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧
    23→{rbrn_runtime}

    25→Container runtime for service deployment.

    27→{rbrn_runtime_docker}

    30→
    31→// ⟦axhrgb_group⟧
    32→{rbrn_group_ark_reference}

    37→// ⟦axhrgv_variable⟧

    57→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧

    61→
    62→// ⟦axhrgb_group⟧
    63→{rbrn_group_entry}
    64→
    65→// ⟦axhrgc_gate⟧
    66→{rbrn_entry_mode}
    67→{rbrn_entry_mode_enabled}

    69→Variables in this group apply only when
    70→{rbrn_entry_mode}
    71→is
    72→{rbrn_entry_mode_enabled}.

    74→// ⟦axhrgv_variable⟧

    88→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧

    93→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧

    99→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧
   100→{rbrn_enclave_sentry_ip}

   104→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧

   125→// ⟦axhrgb_group⟧

   142→// ⟦axhrgb_group⟧

   145→// ⟦axhrgc_gate⟧
   146→{rbrn_uplink_dns_mode}
   147→{rbrn_uplink_dns_mode_allowlist}
   148→
   149→Variables in this group apply only when
   150→{rbrn_uplink_dns_mode}
   151→is
   152→{rbrn_uplink_dns_mode_allowlist}.
   153→
   154→// ⟦axhrgv_variable⟧
   155→{rbrn_uplink_allowed_domains}
   156→
   157→Domain names allowed for bottle-initiated DNS resolution.

   159→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧
   160→{rbrn_volume_mounts}

   162→Podman volume mount arguments for the bottle container.

   165→// ⟦axhro_kindle⟧
   166→{rbkro_kindle}
   167→
   168→Sets defaults for all RBRN_ variables via `VAR="${VAR:-}"` pattern.
   169→Detects unexpected RBRN_ variables via compgen prefix scan.
   170→Builds ZRBRN_ROLLUP serialization string and ZRBRN_DOCKER_ENV injection array.
   171→
   172→// ⟦axhro_validate⟧
   173→{rbkro_validate}
   174→
   175→Validates fields via buv_env_* type checkers.
   176→Validates RBRN_RUNTIME, RBRN_ENTRY_MODE, RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE, RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE enum constraints.
   177→Conditional validation: entry ports when ENTRY_MODE=enabled, allowlists when *_MODE=allowlist.
   178→
   179→// ⟦axhro_render⟧
   180→{rbkro_render}
   181→
   182→Renders via rbcr_* shared module with gate-aware section suppression.
   183→Sections: Core Service Identity, Container Image Configuration, Entry Service Configuration (gated), Enclave Network, Uplink Core, DNS Allowlist (gated), Access Allowlist (gated), Volume Mount.
   184→Displays unexpected variables in red.
   185→Validates after full render completes.
   186→
   187→// ⟦axhro_list⟧
   188→{rbkro_list}
   189→
   190→Enumerates available nameplate instances by scanning assignment files matching the RBRN naming pattern.
   191→Returns moniker identifiers suitable for instance selection.

   193→// ⟦axhro_survey⟧
   194→{rbkro_survey}
   195→
   196→Read-only cross-instance summary of all nameplates.
   197→Iterates rbrn_list, kindles each instance in a subshell to avoid sentinel conflicts.
   198→Displays network topology, entry configuration, and local image availability for all instances.
   199→
   200→// ⟦axhro_audit⟧
   201→{rbkro_audit}
   202→
   203→Cross-instance validation of all nameplates plus external verification.
   204→Performs survey display, then validates cross-instance constraints: port uniqueness, subnet non-overlap, IP uniqueness.
   205→Includes GCB quota headroom check via Service Usage API.

1→// ⟦axhrb_regime⟧
     2→{rbrp_regime}
     3→
     4→This regime specifies variables that define administrative payor configuration within the Recipe Bottle system.
     5→Each
     6→{rbrp_regime}
     7→instance represents the administrative control plane that creates and manages depot projects, links billing accounts, and configures OAuth authentication.
     8→RBRP assignment files are found at the project root (`rbrp.env`) and configure payor project identification, billing account linkage, and OAuth client settings.
     9→
    10→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧
    11→{rbrp_payor_project_id}
    12→
    13→The GCP project ID hosting the OAuth 2.0 client configuration for payor operations.
    14→Must match the global payor project naming pattern.
    15→Required; regex-validated against RBGC_GLOBAL_PAYOR_REGEX.
    16→
    17→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧
    18→{rbrp_billing_account_id}
    19→
    20→GCP billing account identifier linked to newly created depot projects.
    21→Optional during initial setup; format XXXXXX-XXXXXX-XXXXXX when present.
    22→
    23→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧
    24→{rbrp_oauth_client_id}
    25→
    26→Public OAuth 2.0 client identifier for the payor desktop application.
    27→Optional during initial setup; must end with `.apps.googleusercontent.com` when present.
    28→
    29→// ⟦axhro_kindle⟧
    30→{rbkro_kindle}
    31→
    32→Requires RBGC to be kindled first (uses RBGC_GLOBAL_PAYOR_REGEX for validation).
    33→Validates RBRP_PAYOR_PROJECT_ID presence and format.
    34→Validates RBRP_BILLING_ACCOUNT_ID format when present.
    35→Validates RBRP_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID format when present.
    36→
    37→// ⟦axhro_validate⟧
    38→{rbkro_validate}
    39→
    40→RBRP kindle performs validation inline — no separate validate_fields function.
    41→The kindle function is the validator for this regime.
    42→
    43→// ⟦axhro_render⟧
    44→{rbkro_render}
    45→
    46→Renders via rbcr_* shared module.
    47→Sections: Payor Project Identity, Billing Configuration, OAuth Configuration.
    48→Validates after full render completes.
    49→

1→// ⟦axhrb_regime⟧
     2→{rbrs_regime}
     3→
     4→This regime specifies variables that define workstation-specific Podman VM configuration within the Recipe Bottle system.
     5→The
     6→{rbrs_regime}
     7→is a singleton regime containing platform-specific paths and settings for the local development environment.
     8→RBRS assignment files are external configuration files specific to each developer workstation.
     9→
    10→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧
    11→{rbrs_podman_root_dir}
    12→
    13→Root directory for Podman machine storage on the local workstation.
    14→Required; filesystem path string.
    15→
    16→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧
    17→{rbrs_vmimage_cache_dir}
    18→
    19→Directory for caching VM images used by Podman machine initialization.
    20→Required; filesystem path string.
    21→
    22→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧
    23→{rbrs_vm_platform}
    24→
    25→Platform architecture identifier for the Podman VM (e.g., aarch64, x86_64).
    26→Required; string.
    27→
    28→// ⟦axhro_kindle⟧
    29→{rbkro_kindle}
    30→
    31→Validates all 3 RBRS variables inline during kindle.
    32→Uses buv_env_string for all fields.
    33→
    34→// ⟦axhro_validate⟧
    35→{rbkro_validate}
    36→
    37→RBRS kindle performs validation inline — no separate validate_fields function.
    38→The kindle function is the validator for this regime.
    39→
    40→// ⟦axhro_render⟧
    41→{rbkro_render}
    42→
    43→Renders via rbcr_* shared module.
    44→Section: Podman Configuration.
    45→No security-sensitive fields; all values displayed normally.
    46→Validates after full render completes.
    47→

1→// ⟦axhrb_regime⟧
     2→{rbra_regime}
     3→
     4→This regime specifies variables that define service account authentication credentials within the Recipe Bottle system.
     5→Each
     6→{rbra_regime}
     7→instance contains the minimum identity and signing material needed for a specific service account role to authenticate to GCP APIs.
     8→RBRA assignment files are external credential files stored outside the repository, referenced by RBRR variables (RBRR_GOVERNOR_RBRA_FILE, RBRR_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE, RBRR_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE).
     9→
    10→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧
    11→{rbra_client_email}
    12→
    13→The service account email address assigned by GCP, used as the issuer (iss) when constructing JWTs for OAuth2 token exchange.
    14→Required; must match `*.iam.gserviceaccount.com` pattern.
    15→
    16→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧
    17→{rbra_private_key}
    18→
    19→PEM-encoded RSA private key material corresponding to the service account.
    20→Required; presence-checked for PEM header.
    21→Security-sensitive: never logged, masked in render output.
    22→
    23→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧
    24→{rbra_project_id}
    25→
    26→The GCP project ID that owns the service account.
    27→Required; provides scope for all API operations.
    28→
    29→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧
    30→{rbra_token_lifetime_sec}
    31→
    32→OAuth token lifetime in seconds for JWT token exchange.
    33→Required; integer validated in range 300-3600 (typically 1800).
    34→
    35→// ⟦axhro_kindle⟧
    36→{rbkro_kindle}
    37→
    38→Sets defaults for all RBRA fields.
    39→Detects unexpected RBRA_ variables via compgen.
    40→Builds ZRBRA_ROLLUP with masked private key.
    41→No external regime dependencies for kindling.
    42→
    43→// ⟦axhro_validate⟧
    44→{rbkro_validate}
    45→
    46→Validates RBRA_CLIENT_EMAIL format (service account email pattern).
    47→Validates RBRA_PRIVATE_KEY contains PEM key material (presence only, never logs value).
    48→Validates RBRA_PROJECT_ID as non-empty string.
    49→Validates RBRA_TOKEN_LIFETIME_SEC in range 300-3600.
    50→Dies on unexpected RBRA_ variables.
    51→
    52→// ⟦axhro_render⟧
    53→{rbkro_render}
    54→
    55→Renders via rbcr_* shared module.
    56→Sections: Service Account Identity, Authentication Material, Token Configuration.
    57→RBRA_PRIVATE_KEY displayed as masked (redacted with character count).
    58→Validates after full render completes.
    59→
    60→// ⟦axhro_list⟧
    61→{rbkro_list}
    62→
    63→Enumerates RBRA files referenced by RBRR (governor, retriever, director).
    64→Reports file path and existence status for each role.
    65→

1→// ⟦axhrb_regime⟧
     2→{rbro_regime}
     3→
     4→This regime specifies OAuth 2.0 credentials used by the Payor role for token generation and API authentication.
     5→RBRO assignment files are external credential files stored in ~/.rbw/rbro.env with 600 permissions, never committed to version control.
     6→
     7→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧
     8→{rbro_client_secret}
     9→
    10→OAuth 2.0 client secret from the Google Cloud Console OAuth client configuration.
    11→Required; string validated.
    12→Security-sensitive: masked in render output, never logged.
    13→
    14→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧
    15→{rbro_refresh_token}
    16→
    17→Long-lived OAuth refresh token obtained during the authorization flow.
    18→Used to obtain fresh access tokens for Payor operations.
    19→Required; string validated.
    20→Security-sensitive: masked in render output, never logged.
    21→
    22→// ⟦axhro_kindle⟧
    23→{rbkro_kindle}
    24→
    25→Sets defaults for both RBRO variables.
    26→Detects unexpected RBRO_ variables via compgen.
    27→Builds ZRBRO_ROLLUP with both values masked.
    28→
    29→// ⟦axhro_validate⟧
    30→{rbkro_validate}
    31→
    32→Validates RBRO_CLIENT_SECRET as non-empty string.
    33→Validates RBRO_REFRESH_TOKEN as non-empty string.
    34→Dies on unexpected RBRO_ variables.
    35→
    36→// ⟦axhro_render⟧
    37→{rbkro_render}
    38→
    39→Renders via rbcr_* shared module.
    40→Section: OAuth Credentials.
    41→Both RBRO_CLIENT_SECRET and RBRO_REFRESH_TOKEN displayed as masked (redacted with character count).
    42→Validates after full render completes.

1→// ⟦axhrb_regime⟧
     2→{burd_regime}
     3→
     4→This regime specifies the ephemeral runtime state assembled by bud_dispatch.sh during tabtarget execution.
     5→Unlike file-sourced regimes, BURD variables are constructed inline during dispatch — no assignment file exists.
     6→Variables arrive via three phases: input (caller-set), computed (derived during setup), and parsed (extracted from tabtarget filename).
     7→
     8→// ⟦axhrgb_group⟧
     9→{burd_group_input}
    10→
    11→Variables set by the caller before dispatch invocation.
    12→These configure dispatch behavior (verbosity, logging, interactivity) and identify the regime and coordinator to use.
    13→
    14→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧
    15→{burd_verbose}
    16→
    17→Verbosity level controlling diagnostic output.
    18→0=silent, 1=show messages, 2=enable bash trace (set -x).
    19→Defaults to 0 if unset.
    20→
    21→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧
    22→{burd_regime_file}
    23→
    24→Path to the BURC regime assignment file to source during setup.
    25→Provides project-level configuration for tabtarget infrastructure.
    26→
    27→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧
    28→{burd_no_log}
    29→
    30→When non-empty, suppresses all log file creation and output.
    31→Used for lightweight dispatch where logging overhead is unwanted.
    32→
    33→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧
    34→{burd_interactive}
    35→
    36→When non-empty, enables interactive mode: output goes to terminal via tee rather than curated log pipeline.
    37→
    38→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧
    39→{burd_color}
    40→
    41→Color output policy.
    42→Input as auto/0/1; resolved to 0 or 1 during dispatch.
    43→Respects NO_COLOR environment convention.
    44→
    45→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧
    46→{burd_coordinator_script}
    47→
    48→Path to the coordinator script (workbench/testbench) that handles the dispatched command.
    49→
    50→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧
    51→{burd_launcher}
    52→
    53→Path to the launcher bootstrap script, set by the tabtarget before exec.
    54→Identifies which launcher mediates between the tabtarget and the dispatch system.
    55→
    56→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧
    57→{burd_station_file}
    58→
    59→Absolute path to the developer's station regime assignment file.
    60→Set by the launcher from BURC_STATION_FILE, resolved to an absolute path.
    61→Used by workbenches as a guard to ensure proper launcher invocation.
    62→
    63→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧
    64→{burd_term_cols}
    65→
    66→Terminal column width at dispatch time.
    67→Detected from stty if available, defaults to 80.
    68→Used by presentation regime for output formatting.
    69→
    70→// ⟦axhrgb_group⟧
    71→{burd_group_computed}
    72→
    73→Variables derived during dispatch setup by zbud_setup and zbud_process_args.
    74→These provide temporal identity, filesystem paths for ephemeral artifacts, and execution context.
    75→
    76→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧
    77→{burd_now_stamp}
    78→
    79→Unique timestamp combining date, time, PID, and random component.
    80→Provides temporal identity for the dispatch invocation.
    81→
    82→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧
    83→{burd_temp_dir}
    84→
    85→Ephemeral directory created for this dispatch invocation.
    86→Removed after execution; contains working artifacts.
    87→
    88→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧
    89→{burd_output_dir}
    90→
    91→Fixed output directory cleared and recreated on each dispatch.
    92→Contains command results accessible after execution.
    93→
    94→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧
    95→{burd_transcript}
    96→
    97→Path to the transcript file within the temporary directory.
    98→Records execution trace.
    99→
   100→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧
   101→{burd_git_context}
   102→
   103→Git describe output capturing repository state at dispatch time.
   104→Recorded in logs for traceability.
   105→
   106→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧
   107→{burd_log_last}
   108→
   109→Path to the "last" log file, overwritten each dispatch.
   110→Quick access to most recent execution output.
   111→
   112→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧
   113→{burd_log_same}
   114→
   115→Path to the "same" log file for the command+target combination.
   116→Curated (colors stripped, temp paths normalized, VOLATILE lines removed).
   117→
   118→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧
   119→{burd_log_hist}
   120→
   121→Path to the timestamped historical log file for the command+target combination.
   122→Includes timestamps on each line.
   123→
   124→// ⟦axhrgb_group⟧
   125→{burd_group_parsed}
   126→
   127→Variables extracted from the tabtarget filename during argument processing.
   128→The tabtarget inscription is split on the configured delimiter into positional tokens.
   129→
   130→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧
   131→{burd_command}
   132→
   133→Primary command token (first token from tabtarget split).
   134→Legacy alias equivalent to BURD_TOKEN_1.
   135→
   136→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧
   137→{burd_target}
   138→
   139→Complete tabtarget string as received by dispatch.
   140→Preserved for logging and diagnostics.
   141→
   142→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧
   143→{burd_cli_args}
   144→
   145→Array of additional CLI arguments passed after the tabtarget.
   146→Forwarded to the coordinator command.
   147→
   148→// ⟦axhrv_variable⟧
   149→{burd_token_1}
   150→{burd_token_2}
   151→{burd_token_3}
   152→{burd_token_4}
   153→{burd_token_5}
   154→
   155→Positional tokens 1-5 from tabtarget filename split.
   156→Token 1 is the colophon, token 2 is the frontispiece, token 3 is the imprint (if present).
   157→Tokens default to empty string if not present in the inscription.
   158→
   159→// ⟦axhro_kindle⟧
   160→{bukro_kindle}
   161→
   162→Inline initialization within zbud_main: zbud_setup sources the regime file, derives computed variables, creates ephemeral directories.
   163→zbud_process_args parses the tabtarget and sets parsed variables.
   164→No separate kindle function — the dispatch main IS the kindle.
   165→
   166→// ⟦axhro_validate⟧
   167→{bukro_validate}
   168→
   169→Unexpected BURD_ variable detection via compgen scan after all variables are set.
   170→Dies on any variable not in the known set.
   171→Sentinel (zburd_sentinel) allows downstream code to assert dispatch is properly initialized.
